questions.stem: Which organization-level role can create new Snowflake accounts in an organization?
questions.explanation: ORGADMIN operates at the organization scope and is used for account-level organization administration.

questions.stem: Which command changes the current role for a session?
questions.explanation: USE ROLE activates a different role in the current session.

questions.stem: A team wants separate billing and administration boundaries for two business units. What is the best high-level design?

options.text: Two separate accounts within one organization
One role per business unit only

questions.explanation: Separate accounts give clearer isolation boundaries for billing, security, and administration than only roles or schemas.

questions.stem: Which feature helps centrally view usage across multiple accounts in one organization?
questions.explanation: Organization-level usage views help administrators analyze consumption across accounts.

questions.stem: What is a main use case for a failover group?

options.text: Loading files from an external stage
Replicating and failing over groups of account objects for business continuity

questions.explanation: Failover groups support disaster recovery by replicating supported objects and enabling failover.

questions.stem: Why might an administrator use account parameters?

options.text: To configure account-wide behavior such as session policies or feature defaults
To store warehouse query results forever

questions.explanation: Account parameters control behavior defaults and governance settings at the account scope.

questions.stem: Which built-in role is commonly associated with creating and managing users?
questions.explanation: USERADMIN focuses on user and role management tasks.

questions.stem: A company wants all development resources isolated from production administration. Which pattern is strongest?

options.text: Separate accounts for dev and prod

questions.explanation: Separate accounts provide stronger isolation for credentials, policies, and billing than only splitting warehouses or databases.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: account design best practices

questions.stem: What is the safest reason to avoid using ACCOUNTADMIN for day-to-day analyst work?

options.text: It has broad privileges that violate least privilege principles

questions.explanation: ACCOUNTADMIN is very powerful, so routine work should use narrower roles aligned to least privilege.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: access control best practices

questions.stem: Which objects can be included in business continuity replication strategies? (Select 2.)

options.text: Shares and account-level disaster recovery objects via failover groups

questions.explanation: Replication and failover features cover supported databases and related objects, not UI preferences.

questions.stem: What does a resource monitor primarily track?
questions.explanation: Resource monitors watch credit usage and can trigger notifications or suspension actions.

questions.stem: Which action can a resource monitor take after a credit threshold is reached?
questions.explanation: Resource monitors can notify and optionally suspend workloads when thresholds are exceeded.

questions.stem: When are multi-cluster warehouses most useful?

options.text: For preventing users from creating roles
For handling bursts of concurrent queries

questions.explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses help handle concurrency by adding clusters when queueing increases.

questions.stem: Which setting most directly helps reduce wasted credits on an infrequently used warehouse?
questions.explanation: Auto-suspend stops idle compute so credits are not consumed unnecessarily.

questions.stem: What does queueing on a warehouse usually indicate first?

options.text: Concurrency pressure or under-sized compute for the workload

questions.explanation: Queueing usually points to compute pressure, concurrency limits, or warehouse sizing issues.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: warehouse performance and queueing

questions.stem: Which view or area is most helpful for understanding per-query compute behavior?
questions.explanation: Query history and profile show execution details, scan volume, and bottlenecks.

questions.stem: Which settings are directly relevant when configuring a multi-cluster warehouse? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Minimum and maximum cluster counts define how a multi-cluster warehouse scales.

questions.stem: What is the main difference between standard and economy scaling policies for multi-cluster warehouses?

options.text: Economy tries to minimize cluster usage, while standard adds clusters more aggressively for performance

questions.explanation: Economy favors cost savings, while standard is more aggressive about concurrency performance.

questions.stem: Which warehouse size change most directly gives a single query more compute resources?

options.text: Increasing warehouse size from Small to Large

questions.explanation: Larger warehouses provide more compute resources to execute workloads.

questions.stem: Which statement about serverless features is correct?

options.text: They always use a customer-managed warehouse
Snowflake manages the compute, but usage still contributes to cost
They are free if a warehouse is suspended
They cannot be monitored in account usage

questions.explanation: Serverless features still incur cost even though Snowflake manages the underlying compute.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: serverless features and cost

questions.stem: A data team wants a warning before credits are exhausted, without immediately stopping jobs. What should they configure?

options.text: A resource monitor with a notify threshold

questions.explanation: A notify threshold provides early warning before a more severe suspension action is needed.

questions.stem: Which warehouse behavior is most likely improved by enabling additional clusters rather than simply resizing one cluster?

options.text: High concurrency of many medium queries

questions.explanation: Additional clusters mainly help concurrency, whereas a single bigger cluster helps individual query performance.

questions.stem: What does AUTO_RESUME=TRUE allow?

options.text: A suspended warehouse to restart automatically when queries arrive

questions.explanation: Auto-resume restarts compute when new work is submitted.

questions.stem: Which statement about role hierarchy is correct?

options.text: Privileges flow from child roles to parent roles
Roles can inherit privileges from other roles granted to them
Users cannot have more than one role

questions.explanation: Snowflake roles can inherit privileges through role-to-role grants.

questions.stem: Which privilege is needed to allow a role to pass a warehouse to another role?

options.text: USAGE on the warehouse plus OWNERSHIP on the role
OPERATE on the warehouse and MANAGE GRANTS or appropriate grant authority

questions.explanation: Granting privileges requires suitable grant authority, and warehouse access commonly involves OPERATE/USAGE depending on the action.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: grants and grant authority

questions.stem: What does future grants help administrators do?

options.text: Apply grants automatically to objects created later in a schema or database
Share future data to all consumers

questions.explanation: Future grants reduce manual work by automatically applying privileges to new objects.

questions.stem: Which privileges are commonly required just to query a table? (Select 2.)

options.text: SELECT on the table or view

questions.explanation: A role typically needs USAGE on parent containers and SELECT on the object being queried.

questions.stem: Why is OWNERSHIP treated specially in Snowflake?

options.text: It confers full control over an object and is transferred rather than simply duplicated
It is only for users, not roles

questions.explanation: OWNERSHIP is the highest object privilege and is transferred carefully because it implies full control.

questions.stem: Which object type is used to apply column masking logic?
questions.explanation: Masking policies are bound to columns to control how sensitive values are exposed.

questions.stem: A secure application needs a procedure to run with the owner’s privileges instead of the caller’s. Which pattern is relevant?
questions.explanation: Owner’s rights procedures can execute with the privileges of the owner, subject to Snowflake’s procedure rules.

questions.stem: What is the purpose of a custom functional role such as ANALYST_READ_ONLY?

options.text: To group grants that match a job function

questions.explanation: Custom roles map privileges to job functions and support least privilege.

questions.stem: Which statement about granting privileges directly to users is most accurate?

options.text: It is recommended for all production environments
It works, but role-based grants scale and audit better
It is required for imported privileges
It eliminates the need for MFA

questions.explanation: Direct user grants are possible in some cases, but roles are more maintainable and auditable.

questions.stem: When a role needs to query a secure view, which additional requirement is often true beyond SELECT on the view?

options.text: USAGE on the containing database and schema

questions.explanation: Container object USAGE is still required along the database/schema path.

questions.stem: Which command is used to activate a secondary role set for privilege evaluation in a session?
questions.explanation: ENABLE SECONDARY ROLES controls whether granted secondary roles participate in privilege checks.

questions.stem: What is a common reason to use database roles?

options.text: To scope privileges within a database and make sharing role management more modular

questions.explanation: Database roles localize privilege management within a database boundary.

questions.stem: A team needs row-level filtering by region for different users. Which feature is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Row access policies filter which rows a user can see based on policy logic.

questions.stem: What does a network policy control?
questions.explanation: Network policies govern allowed or blocked client IP ranges.

questions.stem: Which authentication approach is especially common for automated service connections?
questions.explanation: Key-pair auth avoids embedded passwords in many automation scenarios.

questions.stem: What is a major benefit of single sign-on integration?

options.text: Users authenticate through the enterprise identity provider

questions.explanation: SSO centralizes identity and login workflows with the enterprise IdP.

questions.stem: Which statement about MFA is accurate?

options.text: It adds another factor to login verification
It is only available in Business Critical edition

questions.explanation: MFA strengthens interactive authentication by requiring a second factor.

questions.stem: Which controls help reduce unauthorized access risk at login time? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Network restrictions and MFA both reduce login-time attack surface.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: MFA and network policies

questions.stem: Why should administrators be cautious about broad grants to the PUBLIC role?

options.text: PUBLIC is automatically associated with all users

questions.explanation: Because every user gets PUBLIC, broad privileges there widen exposure unnecessarily.

questions.stem: Which capability is associated with customer-managed encryption key participation in Snowflake?
questions.explanation: Tri-Secret Secure adds customer-managed key participation for encryption in supported environments.

questions.stem: What does TLS protect in Snowflake connections?

options.text: Data in transit between clients and Snowflake
Stored table files in cloud storage only

questions.explanation: TLS protects network traffic in transit.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: data encryption in transit

questions.stem: A company wants to restrict a contractor to specific source IP ranges. Which feature should be configured?

options.text: Network policy at the user or account level

questions.explanation: Network policies are the direct mechanism for IP-based access restrictions.

questions.stem: What is a common reason to define a clustering key?

options.text: To improve pruning for large tables with selective filters

questions.explanation: Clustering can improve pruning efficiency on very large tables with predictable filter patterns.

questions.stem: Which cache can let repeated identical queries return without re-executing if underlying data is unchanged?
questions.explanation: The result cache can return prior results when query text and relevant conditions match.

questions.stem: What does Time Travel primarily provide?

options.text: Access to historical table data versions within a retention period

questions.explanation: Time Travel enables querying and restoring historical data within the configured retention window.

questions.stem: Which warehouse action is most appropriate when one very large query needs faster execution?
questions.explanation: A larger warehouse generally gives a single large query more compute resources.

questions.stem: How does zero-copy cloning reduce storage overhead initially?

options.text: It reuses existing underlying storage metadata until changes are made
It exports data to external stages

questions.explanation: Zero-copy cloning initially shares underlying storage metadata and only stores changed data separately later.

questions.stem: Which features can add storage cost beyond active table data itself? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Historical retention and Fail-safe both contribute to storage-related cost.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: storage costs, Time Travel, Fail-safe

questions.stem: What is the most accurate description of search optimization service?

options.text: An optional service that can accelerate selective point lookup-style queries

questions.explanation: Search optimization is a performance feature for selective search patterns, not a warehouse or security feature.

questions.stem: When would an administrator most likely choose a transient table over a permanent table?

options.text: When lower recovery overhead is acceptable for intermediate data
When object ownership cannot be assigned
When the table must support only JSON

questions.explanation: Transient tables are often used for intermediate or staging data where Fail-safe is unnecessary.

questions.stem: A query scans far more micro-partitions than expected. What concept is most relevant to investigate?
questions.explanation: Poor pruning means more micro-partitions must be scanned, which can hurt performance and cost.

questions.stem: Which Snowflake area helps administrators review who ran which queries and when?

options.text: Query history and account usage views

questions.explanation: Query history and account usage views support audit and operational review.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: account usage and query history

questions.stem: Why are access history style views valuable for compliance teams?

options.text: They help analyze which data objects were accessed

questions.explanation: Access history supports auditability by showing how data objects were touched.

questions.stem: Which practice best supports least-privilege compliance?

options.text: Use narrowly scoped custom roles and review grants regularly
Store all files in user stages forever

questions.explanation: Least privilege depends on well-scoped roles and periodic review, not broad administrative grants.

questions.stem: Which objects can be included in business continuity replication strategies? (Select 2.)

options.text: Shares and account-level disaster recovery objects via failover groups

questions.explanation: Replication and failover features cover supported databases and related objects, not UI preferences. A second closely related administrative concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: What does a resource monitor primarily track? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Resource monitors watch credit usage and can trigger notifications or suspension actions. A second closely related administrative concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: Which action can a resource monitor take after a credit threshold is reached? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Resource monitors can notify and optionally suspend workloads when thresholds are exceeded. A second closely related administrative concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: Which organization-level role can create new Snowflake accounts in an organization? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: ORGADMIN operates at the organization scope and is used for account-level organization administration. A second closely related administrative concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: Which command changes the current role for a session? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: USE ROLE activates a different role in the current session. A second closely related administrative concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: A team wants separate billing and administration boundaries for two business units. What is the best high-level design? (Select 2.)

options.text: Two separate accounts within one organization
One role per business unit only

questions.explanation: Separate accounts give clearer isolation boundaries for billing, security, and administration than only roles or schemas. A second closely related administrative concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: Which feature helps centrally view usage across multiple accounts in one organization? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Organization-level usage views help administrators analyze consumption across accounts. A second closely related administrative concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: What is a main use case for a failover group? (Select 2.)

options.text: Loading files from an external stage
Replicating and failing over groups of account objects for business continuity

questions.explanation: Failover groups support disaster recovery by replicating supported objects and enabling failover. A second closely related administrative concept is also correct in this variant.
